RIVERSIDE OFFICE COMPLEX
Project No.: RVR-2026-001
Location: 1200 Riverside Drive, Springfield, IL
Owner: Springfield Development Group
Architect: Hartwell & Associates, AIA
Bid Date: April 30, 2026

PROJECT DESCRIPTION
This project consists of the construction of a new four-story office complex
totaling approximately 48,000 square feet. Work includes site preparation,
structural concrete, structural steel framing, building envelope, interior
finishes, mechanical, and electrical systems.

================================================================================
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE
================================================================================

PART 1 - GENERAL

1.1 SUMMARY
A. This section includes cast-in-place concrete for foundations, slabs-on-
   grade, elevated slabs, columns, beams, and shear walls.

1.2 QUALITY ASSURANCE
A. Concrete strength: 4000 PSI minimum at 28 days for structural elements.
B. Slabs-on-grade: 3500 PSI, 6-inch thickness with #4 rebar at 12 inches on
   center each way.

PART 2 - PRODUCTS

2.1 CONCRETE MIX
A. Normal-weight concrete, unit weight 150 pcf.
B. Maximum aggregate size: 3/4 inch.
C. Water-cement ratio: 0.45 maximum for exterior exposed concrete.

PART 3 - EXECUTION

3.1 QUANTITIES AND PLACEMENT
A. Slab-on-grade: Approximately 500 CY of 4000 PSI concrete across the full
   building footprint (12,000 SF at 6-inch depth).
B. Elevated structural slabs (Levels 2-4): 350 CY post-tensioned concrete.
C. Foundation walls and footings: 180 CY, 5000 PSI concrete.
D. Columns and shear walls: 90 CY, 5000 PSI high-strength mix.
Total estimated concrete: 1,120 CY.

3.2 FORMWORK
A. Provide all shoring, forming, and stripping in accordance with ACI 301.
B. Reshoring required for elevated slabs during construction.

================================================================================
SECTION 05 12 00 - STRUCTURAL STEEL FRAMING
================================================================================

PART 1 - GENERAL

1.1 SUMMARY
A. Structural steel wide-flange columns, beams, girders, and bracing for the
   primary gravity and lateral load resisting system.

1.2 SUBMITTALS
A. Shop drawings signed and sealed by a licensed structural engineer.
B. Mill certifications for all steel shapes.

PART 2 - PRODUCTS

2.1 STEEL MATERIALS
A. Wide-flange shapes: ASTM A992, Grade 50.
B. Hollow structural sections (HSS): ASTM A500, Grade C.
C. Base plates and connection plates: ASTM A36.
D. High-strength bolts: ASTM A325 or A490.

PART 3 - EXECUTION

3.1 QUANTITIES
A. Primary framing (columns, beams, girders): 45 tons structural steel.
B. Miscellaneous steel (angles, plates, embed plates): 8 tons.
C. Metal deck (composite): 14,500 SF, 3-inch composite deck, 20-gauge.
Total structural steel: 53 tons.

3.2 ERECTION
A. Plumb and level all framing within L/1000 of story height.
B. Field-welded connections per AWS D1.1 by certified welders.

================================================================================
SECTION 31 23 16 - EXCAVATION
================================================================================

PART 1 - GENERAL

1.1 SUMMARY
A. Excavation for building foundations, utility trenches, and site grading.

1.2 EXISTING CONDITIONS
A. Soil bearing capacity: 2,000 psf minimum assumed; verify with geotechnical
   report dated January 2026.
B. Groundwater: Expected at 12 feet below existing grade. Dewatering required
   for any work below elevation 578.0.

PART 2 - EXECUTION

2.1 QUANTITIES
A. Bulk excavation for building pad: 2,800 CY (cut) to reach bearing stratum
   at elevation 575.5.
B. Perimeter foundation trenching: 420 LF at average 4 feet wide by 3 feet
   deep = 185 CY.
C. Utility trenching (water, sewer, electrical duct bank): 620 LF total.
D. Excess material to be hauled off-site: estimated 2,200 CY export.

2.2 BACKFILL
A. Structural backfill: compacted in 8-inch lifts to 95% Standard Proctor.
B. Granular backfill (free-draining) required within 5 feet of building
   foundation walls.

NOTE: Hazardous material abatement is NOT included in this contract. Owner to
provide Phase II environmental clearance prior to excavation start. No
asbestos, lead paint, or soil remediation scope is identified.

================================================================================
SECTION 09 91 00 - PAINTING
================================================================================

PART 1 - GENERAL

1.1 SUMMARY
A. Interior and exterior painting including primers and finish coats for all
   painted surfaces.
B. Exterior coating system for metal panels and exposed structural steel.

1.2 QUALITY ASSURANCE
A. All coatings must meet VOC limits per Illinois EPA regulations.
B. Applicator must have minimum 5 years' experience applying specified systems.

PART 2 - PRODUCTS

2.1 INTERIOR PAINT SYSTEM
A. Primer: Sherwin-Williams Preprite ProBlock Interior Latex Primer.
B. Finish (walls): Two coats Sherwin-Williams Duration Interior, Eggshell.
C. Finish (ceilings): Two coats Sherwin-Williams Ceiling Flat.

2.2 EXTERIOR PAINT SYSTEM
A. Metal panels: Kynar 500 fluoropolymer factory-applied coating, 70% PVDF.
B. Exposed structural steel (canopy, parking structure): two-component
   polyurethane, Tnemec Series 1075.

PART 3 - EXECUTION

3.1 QUANTITIES
A. Interior wall painting: approximately 38,000 SF of gypsum board walls,
   2 coats finish after 1 coat primer.
B. Interior ceiling painting: 12,000 SF exposed concrete deck, 2 coats.
C. Exterior steel: 1,200 SF exposed structural members.
D. Door frames and miscellaneous metals: 180 EA frames plus miscellaneous.

3.2 SURFACE PREPARATION
A. Clean all surfaces free of dust, oil, and contaminants before priming.
B. Fill and sand all fastener holes and imperfections in gypsum board.
C. Prime all new drywall within 24 hours of taping completion.

================================================================================
END OF SPECIFICATIONS
================================================================================

NOTE FOR GAP ANALYSIS:
The following standard CSI divisions are intentionally omitted from this
abbreviated specification set and should be flagged during gap analysis:
- Division 07 (Thermal and Moisture Protection — no roofing, waterproofing, or
  insulation sections included)
- Division 08 (Openings — no door, window, or glazing specifications provided)
- Division 23 (HVAC — mechanical systems specifications not included)
- Division 26 (Electrical — electrical systems specifications not included)

These omissions represent genuine scope gaps that the APEX system should
identify and flag for the estimator's attention.
